channel.title: Fish on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Fish on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Smoked Whitefish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups water
  • 2/3 cup sugar
  • ½ cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup pickling salt
  • 1/3 cup soya sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 tbsp pepper
  • 2 bay leaves, crushed

Instructions

In 5-qt glass or plastic container, combine brine ingredients. Stir until sugar and salt are dissolved. Add fillets to brine. Cover and refrigerate 12 hours or overnight. Drain; discard brine from fillets. Rinse with water. Pat dry with paper towels. Arrange fillets on cooling racks. Aair dry for 1 hour or until fillets are shiny and dry.

item.title: Salmon Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15-1/2-oz tin canned salmon
  • 1 cup liquid (liquid from salmon plus milk)
  • ½ cup soft bread crumbs
  • 1 tsp grated onions
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • ½ tsp garlic salt

Instructions

Flake salmon and add salmon liquid and milk. Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.

Pour into a greased 8-inch pie pan and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 – 50 minutes or until center is solid when tested with a knife.

item.title: Salmon Bisque
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 Tbsp butter
  • 3-1/2 cups milk
  • 1 onion, finely chopped
  • 7-1/2 oz tin salmon of 1 qt canned lake trout
  • 1 stalk celery, chopped
  • salt and pepper
  • 3 Tbsp flour

Instructions

Melt butter in a saucepan and sauté onion and celery. Add flour and stir well. Add milk slowly, stirring constantly. Add salmon and season with salt and pepper. Cook over low heat until creamy and smooth.

Reheat gently if served a t later date. Serves 4. Makes a hearty first course or a full meal when served with fresh biscuits and salad.

item.title: Mary's Guide Service Secret Smoked Fish Recipe
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups water
  • 2 cups soya sauce
  • 2 cups apple juice
  • 1 cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup pickling salt
  • 2 tbsp whole black peppercorns
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tsp onion powder
  • 3 lbs lake trout, or substitute fillets (8 oz each), 1 inch thick, skin on

Instructions

Same instructions as recipe for Smoked Whitefish.

item.title: Fish Scallop
item.description:
• 4 large potatoes
  • 1 tsp thyme
  • 2 medium onions
  • ¼ tsp basil
  • 1 to 1-1/2 lb fish fillets, deboned
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper

SAUCE:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup butter
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup flour
  • Dash of pepper
  • 2 cup milk
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • 1 cup grated Cheddar cheese (or Cheez Whiz)

Instructions

Boil 4 large potatoes until almost cooked. Slice and layer in a greased 9x13-inch dish. Slice a thin layer of onion over potato. Place a layer of thinly sliced fillet (uncooked & deboned) over.

item.title: Fake Salmon
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 7 lbs chopped pike
  • 1 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 cup vinegar
  • 1 cup tomato soup
  • 1-1/2 tbsp salt
  • 1 tbsp pepper garlic powder – optional
  • Parsley – optional

Instructions

Mix last seven ingredients together. Pour over chopped fish and leave in refrigerator overnight.

In the morning, bake at 250 degrees for 5 hours. Pack in sterilized jars.

item.title: Cheesy Baked Catfish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup crushed cheese-flavored crackers
  • ¼ cup sesame seed
  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 1 tbsp snipped fresh parsley
  • 2-1/4 lbs catfish fillets, skin removed
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tbsp shredded fresh Parmesan cheese
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ¼ tsp cayenne

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 F. In shallow dish, combine crackers, sesame seed, parsley, salt, pepper and cayenne. Set aside.

In 1-quart saucepan, melt margarine over medium heat. Pour into another shallow dish. Dip each fillet first in margarine and then in crumb mixture, pressing lightly to coat.

item.title: Cheese Fish Bake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb fish fillets (Cod)
  • 1/2 cup shredded Cheddar cheese
  • 1 can cream of mushroom soup
  • Dash of paprika
  • Salt and Pepper

Instructions

Arrange fish in greased shallow baking dish and stir soup over fish. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cheese. Bake at 375 degrees for 45 minutes.

item.title: Blackened Fish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp paprika
  • 2-1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp onion powder
  • 1 tsp garlic power
  • 1 tsp cayenne
  • ¾ tsp white pepper
  • ¾ tsp black pepper
  • ½ tsp dried thyme leaves, crushed
  • ½ tsp dried oregano leaves
  • 2-1/4 lbs catfish, lake trout or substitute fillets (6 oz each) skin removed, cut in half crosswise.

Instructions

Build a campfire and allow it to burn down to glowing coals. In shallow bowl, combine seasoning ingredients. Set aside. Place cast-iron skillet on cooking grate over campfire. When drop of water flicked onto skillet dances across it, skillet is hot enough. Dredge both sides of each fillet in seasoning mixture.

item.title: Batter For Fish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup buttermilk
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp white pepper
  • 1-1/2 cup cornmeal
  • ½ cup flour

item.title: B. B. Q. Fish Coating
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup mayo
  • ¼ cup white wine
  • l tbsp lemon juice
  • l clove garlic, crushed
  • l/4 tsp cayenne pepper
  • Thyme and dill, optional

item.title: Arthur Treacher-Style Fish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 lbs fish fillet
  • Dairy buttermilk (enough to cover)
  • 1 lemon, sliced
  • 1 pint (8 oz) oil for frying
  • Dry pancake mix (for dredging)
  • 2 cups pancake mix
  • 2-1/2 cups club soda

Instructions

Soak fish fillets in buttermilk. Place lemon slices over them, cover, and refrigerate 2–3 hours to remove “fishy” taste.

Heat oil in a heavy 2-1/2 quart saucepan.

Drain off milk and cut fillets in halves (triangle shape). Dredge in dry pancake mix.
